steps.title: Understand the claim and what the table shows
steps.content: The student argues that all areas need more shaded bus-stop shelters, supporting this by pointing out that the highest percentage of bus stops with shaded shelter for any area is "only ______." The word "only" signals the number should be small enough to justify the claim. The correct answer must be the actual highest percentage in the table.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice, then confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 50%.: 50% does not appear anywhere in the table's percentages of shaded bus stops. ✗
  • Choice B — 15%.: 15% is the lowest percentage in the table, not the highest — misidentifies which value the student is citing. ✗
  • Choice C — 90%.: 90% does not appear anywhere in the table's percentages of shaded bus stops. ✗
  • Choice D — 29%.: 29% is the highest percentage of shaded bus stops shown in the table — and calling it "only 29%" effectively supports the student's argument that more shaded shelters are needed everywhere. ✓
That is the correct answer.
